teaser.title: Is It Possible to Leave the Healthcare System to the Private Sector?

about.heading: Ayberk Ceyran
about.paragraph1: I was born in 2007 in Ankara, though my family is originally from Hekimhan, Malatya. I have a broad range of interests at the intersection of economics, software, data analysis, and artificial intelligence. I focus in particular on software development with Python, data science, and web technologies, continually advancing my technical skills. I'm currently preparing for the SAT toward my academic goals, and I aim to continue my education in economics and data analytics in Canada.
about.paragraph2: I designed this website as a digital portfolio bringing together my projects, my research, and the outputs of my learning process. My goal with the content I share here is both to document my own development and to serve as a resource for people with similar interests.
about.paragraph3: In the long run, I aim to integrate technology and economics to produce projects with real added value, carry out academic research, and contribute to the production of knowledge.
about.paragraph5: Thank you for taking the time to visit my site.

items.title: Open Budget Initiative
items.description: A project making public spending visible to everyone through plain-language, regular reports.

items.title: Neighborhood Councils
items.description: A network of regular meetings where decisions are made in the neighborhood, not behind a desk, and residents have a direct voice.

items.title: Youth Policy Workshop
items.description: An education and mentorship program aiming to make young people founding partners in politics, not just spectators.

posts.title: Is It Possible to Leave the Healthcare System to the Private Sector?
posts.description: A comparison of private and public healthcare models — from the US and Cuba to Taiwan's hybrid system, ranked #1 in the world for seven years running.
